ground_truth: Ten years after the Paris Agreement was signed in Le Bourget, France, global CO₂ emissions have reached record levels and temperatures are at 1.45°C above pre-industrial baseline. While the EU has cut emissions by 32%, major emitters like Brazil (deforestation) and India (2070 net zero target) are falling short. The upcoming COP30 summit in Belém, Brazil, is cited as a critical test of international resolve.
summary: In 2024, global temperatures were 1.45°C above pre-industrial levels according to the IPCC. India's emissions are second only to China and the US, with a timeline of 2070 for climate neutrality deemed too late by activists. Lobbyists from oil and gas companies spent $750 million between 2020 and 2024 opposing climate legislation. The upcoming COP30 in Belém in November 2025 will test global commitment to the Paris Agreement.
